items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation improvements for `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet` 1.1.0-beta.1, specifically adding service-specific key concepts, inline code examples, a Samples directory, and descriptions of the new 2025-07-01-preview API version to the README.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet` 1.1.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** README.md and test samples in `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/`
**Scenarios:**
- "Key concepts" section is generic (links to ARM SDK intro only); no ComputeFleet-specific description of fleet resource model, VM profiles, spot priority, or capacity targets
- "Examples" section links to external docs rather than providing inline code snippets
- No `Samples/` directory exists under tests, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` has nothing to inject
- New API version (2025-07-01-preview) introduced in 1.1.0-beta.1 is undescribed in the README

**Root ask:** Add service-specific key concepts, inline snippet placeholders backed by new sample test file (`SampleComputeFleet.cs`), and an API version description to bring the README in line with SDK documentation standards.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue with no runtime bug. The implementation guide in the issue body provides step-by-step instructions.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/README.md` to confirm the current state of the "Key concepts" and "Examples" sections
2. Check generated types in `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/src/Generated/` (e.g., `ComputeFleetData`, `ComputeFleetProperties`, `SpotPriorityProfile`, `ComputeFleetComputeProfile`) before finalizing sample code signatures
3. Create `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/tests/Samples/SampleComputeFleet.cs` with snippet regions as described
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 computefleet` to inject snippets into README placeholders
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — package name starts with `Azure.ResourceManager.`, clearly a management-plane SDK
- **Service:** `Compute - Fleet` — explicitly named package `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet` maps to the `computefleet` service directory; confirmed by CODEOWNERS entry at line 938
- **Confidence:** High — package is explicitly named in the issue title and body; no ambiguity between category or service
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Compute - Fleet %Mgmt` (lines 938–939) — both predicted labels match
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@sahilarora92`
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` label; no assignment (ServiceOwners path); `@mentioned` `@sahilarora92` via mention_owners
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from end of CODEOWNERS; entries for `%ARM - Templates %Mgmt`, `%ARM %Mgmt`, `%Mgmt` (catch-all) were encountered but skipped as they required labels not present; `%Compute - Fleet %Mgmt` at line 938 was first match with all labels present
</details>
